revision.text:
[[ja:泉神社 (米原市)]] {{infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Izumi Shrine | image = [[File:Shrine izumi baibara cty.jpg|250px]]
Izumi Shrine Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|qid=Q824020|lt=Maibara, Shiga|1=Maibara, Shiga}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}} Ōshimizu 35°22'32.1"N 136°24'06.5"E | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|22|32.1|N|136|24|06.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} | Shrine Rank = -- | Established = -- | Main Hall = -- | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Sites = | Annual Festival = -- | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga Prefecture|label=Izumi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[File:Izumijinnjya.jpg|thumb|280px|Izumi Shrine Spring Source]] '''Izumi Shrine''' (泉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|qid=Q824020|lt=Maibara, Shiga|1=Maibara, Shiga}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. == Deities and Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * The shrine possesses a bronze bell designated as a cultural property of Shiga Prefecture. * It also houses a lion mask and a large drum used for rain prayer rituals, both designated as cultural properties of Maibara City. == Izumi Shrine Spring == According to shrine tradition, {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} designated this site as a training ground for archery and horseback riding. When people settled in the area, a sacred spring emerged, which was named "Tensenjo" (Heavenly Spring Place). It was later renamed "Ōshimizu." The spring is one of the "Three Famous Waters of Ibuki" and is associated with legends such as the "Water of Awakening," which revived {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}} during his battle against the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|qid=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}} near {{ill|Mount Ibuki|en|Mount Ibuki|qid=Q2624360|lt=Mount Ibuki|1=Mount Ibuki}}. It is also linked to the legend of {{ill|Oguri Hangan|ja|小栗判官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguri Hangan|1=Oguri Hangan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11462010}}, who prayed for recovery from illness at this site. The place name was later changed to Ōshimizu. The water that springs from the precincts is groundwater that has percolated through limestone forming {{ill|Mount Ibuki|en|Mount Ibuki|qid=Q2624360|lt=Mount Ibuki|1=Mount Ibuki}}, creating small-scale {{ill|Solutional cave|en|Solutional cave|qid=Q7558985|lt=limestone caves|1=Solutional cave}} before surfacing naturally after long-term underground retention.[http://www.gsj.jp/Info/100mt/ibukisan/ibukisan.html Mount Ibuki] - [http://www.gsj.jp/HomePageJP.html Geological Survey of Japan] - {{ill|National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology|en|National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology|qid=Q1076542|lt=National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology|1=National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology}} The average water temperature is 11°C, with an output of 4,500 tons per day. The original spring is off-limits, but a water collection site downstream attracts many visitors. In {{ill|1985|en|1985|qid=Q2431|lt=1985|1=1985}} (Showa 60), it was selected as one of the {{ill|100 Famous Springs of Japan|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Famous Springs of Japan|qq=}}.[http://www2.env.go.jp/water/mizu-site/meisui/data/index.asp?info=53 Izumi Shrine Spring] - [http://www2.env.go.jp/water/mizu-site/meisui/index.html 100 Famous Springs (link outdated)] - {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|en|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|qid=Q1125558|lt=Ministry of the Environment|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)}} == Access == * About 10 minutes by {{ill|Ōmi Bus|ja|近江バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近江鉄道バス|1=Ohmi Railway Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q129172682}} from {{ill|Ōmi-Nagaoka Station|en|Ōmi-Nagaoka Station|qid=Q872483|lt=Ōmi-Nagaoka Station|1=Ōmi-Nagaoka Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|qid=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (get off at "Sugisawa" and walk 20 minutes). * Accessible via {{ill|Shiga Prefectural Route 531 Fujikawa-Harusue Line|ja|滋賀県道531号藤川春照線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefectural Route 531 Fujikawa-Harusue Line|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 531|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11564923}}. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|100 Famous Springs of Japan|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Famous Springs of Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Mount Ibuki|en|Mount Ibuki|qid=Q2624360|lt=Mount Ibuki|1=Mount Ibuki}} {{Shinto}} {{100 Famous Springs of Japan}} {{translated page|ja|泉神社 (米原市)|version=87120904|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:泉神社]] [[ja:泉神社 (米原市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11554898}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Japan's Top 100 Exquisite Waters]] [[Category:Landforms of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Springs of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
